metadata.seed: When people give generic answers to your problems.. Anyone else hate when you're venting to someone about your current struggles and all they say back in response is: "hugs~" or "I don't know what to say, but that sucks" my best friend, who also struggles with mental illness, does it everytime I vent and it annoys me and I don't know why. On one hand I know people's words can't fix you or make everything better, but on the other it'd be nice to at least have someone to talk to (who isn't a therapist) that gets it and actually engages with you from time to time.

patient.query: When people give generic answers to your problems.. Anyone else hate when you're venting to someone about your current struggles and all they say back in response is: "hugs~" or "I don't know what to say, but that sucks" my best friend, who also struggles with mental illness, does it everytime I vent and it annoys me and I don't know why. On one hand I know people's words can't fix you or make everything better, but on the other it'd be nice to at least have someone to talk to (who isn't a therapist) that gets it and actually engages with you from time to time.

triggers: best friend responds with 'hugs~' or 'I don't know what to say, but that sucks'
automatic_thoughts: people's words can't fix my problems
I want someone to engage with me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)
Tends to be critical of others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds really frustrating when you're trying to connect with someone and their response doesn't feel supportive – what do you think it is that you're hoping for in those moments when you're venting?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really frustrating when you're sharing something vulnerable and the response feels dismissive or unhelpful, especially from someone you expect to understand. It's completely valid to feel annoyed and want a more supportive connection.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds really frustrating when you're trying to connect with someone and their response doesn't feel supportive – what do you think it is that you're hoping for in those moments when you're venting?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: best friend responds with 'hugs~' or 'I don't know what to say, but that sucks'

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: people's words can't fix my problems

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds really frustrating when you're trying to connect with someone and their response feels dismissive, especially when you're sharing something vulnerable. Have you considered that maybe your friend feels awkward or doesn't know how to respond to your struggles, and their "hugs~" or "that sucks" is their way of trying to offer comfort, even if it doesn't feel supportive to you?

safety_check.response: It sounds really frustrating to feel unheard, especially when reaching out to someone you trust. When you said you don't know why this bothers you, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?
